sections.paragraph: an unused root (whence זֶפֶת pitch), which I suppose to have had the signification of flowing or pouring, and hence to have been applied to fluid or fusible materials, as is the case with many words springing from the stock sap, sp as סָבָא, שָׂפָה, שָׂפָם, سفح, Arabic وذف to flow, to become liquid, and ودف to become liquid, to melt into drops; in western languages, σπέω, spuo, spuma, sapa, sapo; fpenen, Speichel, Saft, etc. [In the Thes. זֶפֶת is referred to זוּף as its root, hence this supposed root is altogether omitted.]
